news.title: In a vote of confidence for Meta’s Threads, Kalshi adds sharing feature
news.summary: Kalshi now offers a share option that will automatically embed the relevant prediction market chart into a Threads post.

news.title: Google and Tesla think we’re managing the electrical grid all wrong
news.summary: The duo have teamed up with other companies to launch Utilize, which seeks to change how the grid is used and regulated.

news.title: Amazon launches its healthcare AI assistant on its website and app
news.summary: Health AI can answer questions, explain health records, manage prescription renewals, book appointments, and more.

news.title: DOGE employee stole Social Security data and put it on a thumb drive, report says
news.summary: A whistleblower is accusing a former DOGE member of stealing a large number of Americans’ personal data while he was working at the Social Security Administration, with the plan of using it at his new job.

news.title: ‘Pokémon Pokopia’ is a game about rehabilitating a broken world — and I love it
news.summary: The latest Pokémon game is a cozy life simulator like "Animal Crossing" or "Minecraft," but in a way that feels more grounded to our actual world.

news.title: Pete Hegseth Is Pushing Defense Employees to Volunteer With DHS
news.summary: The defense secretary has made clear that Pentagon managers are to encourage workers, including civilians, to volunteer to assist in the administration’s immigration crackdown.

news.title: Anker’s Discounted Power Station Can Power Your Next Camping Trip
news.summary: Go off-grid camping for a long weekend with my favorite portable power station from Anker. It’s on sale.

news.title: Interstellar Comet 3I/Atlas Has Another Surprise: It’s Full of Alcohol
news.summary: Analysis of the tail of 3I/Atlas reveals that it contains an anomalous proportion of methanol, a class of alcohol used in fuels.

news.title: DHS Ousts CBP Privacy Officers Who Questioned ‘Illegal’ Orders
news.summary: Department of Homeland Security leaders removed top privacy officers who objected to mislabeling government records to block their public release, WIRED has learned.
